items.title: Bayern and Chelsea eye Verbruggen - Thursday's gossip
items.summary: Bayern Munich will target Bart Verbruggen, John Stones looks set to leave Manchester City, Atletico Madrid are plotting a move for Marc Cucurella, plus more.

items.title: Fan group: Ratcliffe immigrant views 'concerning'
items.summary: A Manchester United supporters group condemned comments made by part-owner Sir Jim Ratcliffe after the British billionaire said the UK has been "colonised" by immigrants.

items.title: Haaland leads Man City win to cut gap to Arsenal
items.summary: Erling Haaland moved joint-fourth on Manchester City's all-time scorer's list as Pep Guardiola's side overpowered Fulham 3-0 to turn up the heat on Arsenal.

items.title: Miami friendly in PR postponed after Messi injury
items.summary: Inter Miami CF's friendly with Independiente del Valle in Puerto Rico has been postponed for two weeks after Lionel Messi sustained a muscle strain in his left hamstring during its last preseason game against Barcelona SC on Saturday.

items.title: USWNT's Bethune off to KC, Hutton joins Bay FC
items.summary: United States midfielder Croix Bethune has joined the Kansas City Current, and USWNT midfielder Claire Hutton has joined Bay FC, in a pair of separate seven-figure moves, multiple sources have confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: Liverpool hand Sunderland 1st Prem home loss
items.summary: Virgil van Dijk 's second-half header saw Liverpool end Sunderland's unbeaten Premier League run at home with a 1-0 win at the Stadium of Light.

items.title: Oxlade-Chamberlain bezorgt Celtic drie punten bij debuut
items.summary: Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain voetbalt weer en dat hebben ze in Schotland geweten ook. In de blessuretijd schoot de voormalig speler van Arsenal en Liverpool zijn nieuwe club Celtic naar de zege op Livingston.

items.title: Mijlpaal voor Van Dijk in wedstrijd tegen Sunderland
items.summary: NEC had woensdagavond de kans om de tweede plek in de Eredivisie te grijpen, maar greep die niet. Eerder op de avond stonden Go Ahead Eagles en SC Heerenveen tegenover elkaar en ook Liverpool kwam in actie. Lees de volledige avond terug in ons blog.

items.title: Sunderland 0-1 Liverpool: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Minute-by-minute updates on the 8.15pm kick-off ⚽ Slot: season ‘worst of my career’ | Email Scott 2 min: … the ball’s played back to Angulo, who tries to trick Alisson at his near post. The keeper reads the danger and claims. That’s got the home fans, already giving it plenty, going some. Meanwhile here’s Peter Oh: “Liverpool have had such bad luck with injuries, shipping comical last-gasp goals, and suspensions. How could things possibly get worse? Running into eleven Black Cats maybe? Sigh.” 1 min: It has been belting down on Wearside all day. It’s still raining. Windy as well. And Sunderland are on the front foot early doors, Mukiele in space on the right and winning a very early corner off Robertson. From which … Continue reading...

items.title: Van Dijk bezorgt Liverpool eerste Premier League-uitzege van 2026
items.summary: Liverpool heeft woensdagavond voor het eerst in 2026 een uitwedstrijd in de Premier League gewonnen. In The Stadium of Light rekende de ploeg van Arne Slot op moeizame wijze af met Sunderland. Een verkeerde inschatting van doelman Robin Roefs zorgde ervoor dat Virgil van Dijk raak kon knikken uit een corner van Mohamed Salah.

items.title: PM tells Ratcliffe to apologise for 'colonised' remarks
items.summary: Sir Keir Starmer said comments by the businessman and Manchester United co-owner were "offensive and wrong".

items.title: Lucca howler is worst of a bad bunch for wasteful Forest against Wolves
items.summary: Evangelos Marinakis was not alone in being flabbergasted by Nottingham Forest failing to convert a six‑on‑one counter-attack. The camera was trained on the club owner, who was visibly furious with the new ­signing Lorenzo Lucca’s appalling finish from 12 yards, sending the ball over, ­blowing the best chance of victory over Wolves. That was one of 35 shots from the hosts who were unable to secure a valuable win in the relegation ­battle. It moves Forest a point further north of West Ham but drawing against the Premier League’s worst side is concerning. The City Ground was tense from the off, knowing the importance of this fixture, with groans and sighs aplenty amid a sloppy performance that brings the focus on to Sean Dyche, who heard the full‑time boos from all around and shouts of “rubbish”. Continue reading...

items.title: Lazio mag dankzij Taylor en Noslin blijven dromen van bekerwinst
items.summary: Lazio heeft woensdagavond in de Coppa Italia afgerekend met Bologna. De nummer acht van de Serie A kwam op achterstand, maar een gouden invalbeurt van Tijjani Noslin zorgde voor de ommekeer. Via strafschoppen plaatste Lazio zich uiteindelijk voor de halve finale.

items.title: Ron Jans vloert NEC met berekende overlevingstactiek
items.summary: Voor FC Utrecht is iets meer dan twintig procent balbezit genoeg geweest om te winnen van NEC: 1-3. Analyse van een wedstrijd waarin Ron Jans met succes kiest voor een pragmatisch strijdplan.

items.title: Late goal sends Villa Park into raptures as Brighton’s miserable run continues
items.summary: On a night when James Milner made history, equalling his former teammate Gareth Barry’s Premier League appearance record, his former club, Aston Villa, created more unwanted headlines for Fabian Hürzeler. A Tyrone Mings header, deflected in by Jack Hinshelwood with four minutes of normal time to play, on the defender’s 200th Villa appearance, was sufficient to down Brighton and extend their miserable run. It said everything about a poor Villa display that Unai Emery was unmoved by the goal. Brighton had looked most likely to score, Ferdi Kadioglu cracking an effort against the crossbar and Milner going close late on. For Hürzeler, the 32-year-old German feeling the heat, it is now one win in their past 13 league matches. Continue reading...

items.title: Manchester City make quick work of extending Fulham’s misery
items.summary: Manchester City’s week is moving along sweetly, this win closing the gap to three points to Arsenal, who travel to Brentford on Thursday. On Sunday, they beat Liverpool at Anfield, on Wednesday they downed Fulham at home, to reel off a 20th consecutive victory against them. The rosiest moment for the title challengers was Erling Haaland’s 39th‑minute strike, a first in the competition from open play in nine games, though Pep Guardiola will be concerned at how City still grasp for supreme control. Continue reading...

items.title: Burnley end 16-game winless run with incredible comeback at Palace
items.summary: Second-bottom Burnley ended their 16-game winless run thanks to a seven-minute Crystal Palace capitulation, which helped Scott Parker's side fight back from two goals down in the first half to win 3-2 at Selhurst Park.

items.title: Broggio scores first senior goal as Falkirk hold on to beat Dundee
items.summary: Ben Broggio marked his full Scottish Premiership debut with what proved to be Falkirk's winning goal after Dundee's Yan Dhanda missed a stoppage-time penalty in their 1-0 defeat.

items.title: Forest held by bottom side Wolves despite having 35 shots
items.summary: Nottingham Forest missed the chance to go five points clear of the relegation zone as they were held to a 0-0 draw by bottom side Wolves, despite having 35 shots at the City Ground.

items.title: De Zerbi, Heitinga and Pochettino the leading contenders after Tottenham sack Frank
items.summary: Heitinga could be interim option to end of season De Zerbi available after leaving Marseille Tottenham are exploring their options after sacking Thomas Frank on Wednesday morning, with Roberto De Zerbi and one of their current coaches, John Heitinga, in the conversation. It is unclear whether the chief executive, Vinai Venkatesham, and the sporting director, Johan Lange, who are overseeing the process, will favour an interim choice which could be Heitinga. If they did, it would buy them time until the summer when other candidates could be available, including Mauricio ­Pochettino and Andoni Iraola. Continue reading...
